title: Differentially expressed genes in oligodendrocytes across brain regions
text:
Code
library(qs2)
library(Seurat)
library(purrr)
library(DESeq2)
library(patchwork)
library(gridExtra)
library(pheatmap)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)


# fgsea: Fast Gene Set Enrichment Analysis
# https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/fgsea/inst/doc/fgsea-tutorial.html
library(fgsea)
# msigdbr: Access to MSigDB gene sets for multiple species
library(msigdbr)

source("20260217-helper_functions.R")


full <- qs_read("seurat_objects/20260217_Part2_fullobj.qs2")
mg   <- subset(full, annotatedclusters == "Oligodendrocyte")

title: Differentially expressed genes in oligodendrocytes across brain regions
text:
Cell counts per region
The table below enumerates the total number of microglial cells captured per brain region and their distribution across treatment groups (aducanumab, Adu; isotype control IgG).


Code
mg@meta.data |>
  group_by(Region) |>
  summarise(
    n_cells = n(),
    n_Adu   = sum(Treatment.Group == "Adu"),
    n_IgG   = sum(Treatment.Group == "IgG")
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(n_cells)) |>
  kbl(caption = "Oligodendrocytes per brain region") |>
  kable_styling("striped")

n_Adu
n_IgG

DefaultAssay(mg) <- "Xenium"
mg   <- subset(mg, !is.na(Region))

title: Differentially expressed genes in endothelial cells across brain regions
Microglia GSEA across brain
text:
Code
library(msigdbr)
library(fgsea)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggrepel)
library(pheatmap)

title: Microglia GSEA across brain
text:
Load M7 gene sets


Code
gene_sets <- msigdbr(species = "Mus musculus", db_species = "MM", collection = "M7")
gene_sets_list <- split(gene_sets$gene_symbol, gene_sets$gs_name)

cat("M7 gene sets loaded:", length(gene_sets_list), "\n")


M7 gene sets loaded: 787

title: Microglia GSEA across brain
text:
Load DE results


Code
de_by_region <- readRDS("results/20260303-DEG_MG_byRegion.rds")

# Panel genes = union of all genes tested across regions
panel_genes <- de_by_region |> map(\(df) rownames(df)) |> purrr::reduce(union)

cat("Regions available:", paste(names(de_by_region), collapse = ", "), "\n")


Regions available: Cortex, Hippocampus, Hypothalamus, Thalamus, WM 


Code
cat("Panel genes:", length(panel_genes), "\n")


Panel genes: 370

title: Microglia GSEA across brain
text:
Panel coverage in M7
The table below quantifies how many panel genes are represented in the M7 collection, and how many M7 gene sets have at least one panel member.


Code
m7_genes <- unique(gene_sets$gene_symbol)

tibble(
  panel_size            = length(panel_genes),
  m7_unique_genes       = length(m7_genes),
  panel_in_m7           = sum(panel_genes %in% m7_genes),
  pct_covered           = round(100 * sum(panel_genes %in% m7_genes) / length(panel_genes), 1),
  genesets_with_overlap = sum(map_int(gene_sets_list, \(g) sum(g %in% panel_genes)) > 0)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Panel coverage in M7 gene sets") |>
  kable_styling("striped")



Panel coverage in M7 gene sets


panel_size
m7_unique_genes
panel_in_m7
pct_covered
genesets_with_overlap




370
5981
274
74.1
690

title: Differentially expressed genes in astrocytes across brain regions
Cell types composition in the full object
text:
Code
library(qs2)
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)


source("20260217-helper_functions.R")


full <- qs_read("seurat_objects/20260217_Part2_fullobj.qs2")
md <- 

md <- full@meta.data 



Cell type composition across brain regions and treatment groups
To characterise the cellular landscape captured by the Xenium 480-gene spatial transcriptomics panel, the total number of cells assigned to each major cell type (annotatedclusters) was quantified per brain region and stratified by treatment group (aducanumab, Adu; isotype control, IgG). Cell type annotations were derived from the seurat full object curated by Chloe


Code
n_cells <- md |>
  group_by(annotatedclusters,Region, Treatment.Group) |>
  summarise(n_cells = n())

ggplot(n_cells, aes(x = Region , y = n_cells, fill = Treatment.Group))+
  geom_col()+
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  facet_wrap(~annotatedclusters, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4)+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(legend.position = "top",
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.


Differential brain effects of anti-Aβ antibody treatment on Amyloid-Related Imaging Abnormalities (ARIA) in a mouse model expressing human APOE4.

Platform: 10x Genomics Xenium In Situ spatial transcriptomics (480-gene targeted panel)
Samples: 6 mouse brains (C57BL/6J with human APOE4 targeted replacement) across 2 slides
Conditions: Aducanumab (anti-Aβ antibody) vs IgG control Slide 1 (0021991): KK4_465 (IgG), KK4_492 (Adu), KK4_504 (IgG) Slide 2 (0021998): KK4_496 (IgG), KK4_502 (Adu), KK4_464 (Adu)




Individual reports: quarto render <file>.qmd




20260217-helper_functions.R — Reusable R functions (fiddle, anayet, scProp)
seurat_objects/ — Post-RCTD/SPLIT Seurat objects (qs2 format, not tracked in git)
results/ — Saved RDS outputs from DE analyses
docs/ — Supporting images (e.g., brain region reference)



Scripts: YYYYMMDD-description.{R|qmd}



Original objects (20260213) had metadata errors and were replaced with corrected 20260217 versions.







File
Contents




20260217_Part2_fullobj.qs2
Full object, all cell types


20260217_Part2_immune.qs2
Immune cell subset


20260225-mg_kai.qs2
Microglia subset (Kai’s annotation, canonical)


20260225-mg_kai_slide{1,2}.qs2
Per-slide microglia


20260217_Part2_fullobj_slide{1,2}.qs2
Per-slide full objects













Column
Values / Notes




sample_ID
KK4_465, KK4_492, KK4_504, KK4_496, KK4_502, KK4_464


Treatment.Group
“Adu” or “IgG”


Region
Brain region (may contain NA)


annotatedclusters
Major cell types (Endothelial, Astrocyte, Oligodendrocyte, OPC, etc.)


annotated_subclusters
Immune subtypes with spaces (e.g., “Activated DAM”, “Border-like Microglia”)


sub.cluster
Microglia subclusters (in Kai’s mg object)







Xenium — Raw counts only (no data layer). Use for pseudobulk DESeq2.
SCT — SCT-normalized. Use for visualization/normalization.
Xenium_SPLIT, SCT_SPLIT — SPLIT-decontaminated versions.





Three main functions, each with a _help() companion:








Function
Purpose
Key packages




fiddle()
Multi-faceted violin plots with colored cluster labels on y-axis
ggplot2, ggtext, grid


anayet()
Differential expression (FindMarkers + DESeq2) with auto-scaled volcano plots
Seurat, EnhancedVolcano


scProp()
Cluster proportion summaries per sample, with optional compareGroups table
ggplot2, compareGroups



Also includes density_decor() for consistent density plot theming.
Default metadata columns expected: final_clusters_clean (cluster), Mouse.ID (sample), Treatment.Group (treatment). Override via function arguments when using objects with different column names.



The DEG qmd files (MG, endothelial, astrocytes, oligo, OPC) share an identical structure:

Load Seurat object → subset to cell type → set Xenium as default assay
Map over brain regions: check replication (≥2 samples/group), aggregate pseudobulk via AggregateExpression(group.by = "sample_ID", return.seurat = TRUE)
Call anayet() with DESeq2 for each region
Summary visualizations: DEG count bar plot, cross-region log2FC heatmap, effect size dot plot
Save results list to results/ as RDS





AggregateExpression() converts underscores to hyphens in column names (KK4_465 → KK4-465). Handle with gsub("_", "-", ...) when mapping treatment metadata.
FindMarkers(test.use = "DESeq2") on SCT assay triggers CheckDots error — use Xenium assay.
Xenium assay only has counts layer (no data). Pseudobulk via AggregateExpression(return.seurat = TRUE) creates both layers automatically.
480-gene panel limits GSEA: use minSize = 5 (not default 15) in fgsea().





Seurat (v5), tidyverse, ggtext, grid, scales, forcats
EnhancedVolcano, compareGroups, speckle, kableExtra
DESeq2, fgsea, msigdbr — for DE and pathway analysis
No renv lockfile — packages managed via library() calls





Use /coding-standard
in-line annotate the code (on top)
do not include package operator :: (dplyr::, purr::). Load the packages if necessary.






All commits must follow this structure:
Title line summarizing the change

Bullet point explaining specific change
Bullet point explaining another change
Additional bullets as needed

The title should be a brief, complete sentence. Leave one blank line between the title and the bullet points. Each bullet point describes a specific change made in the commit. IMPORTANT: do not include messages indicating that the commit has been made by Claude (e.g., “Committed by Claude”, “Claude made changes”, “Generated with Claude Code”, etc.).



Changes to analysis code, new visualizations, parameter adjustments, and data processing steps require bullet points explaining what was done. Changes to documentation, comments, or markdown text can use simple title-only messages without bullets.



Add differential expression analysis for treatment vs control

- Ran DESeq2 with default parameters on normalized counts
- Filtered results for padj < 0.05 and log2FC > 1
- Generated volcano plot saved to figures/volcano_treatment.png
- Exported significant genes to results/DE_genes_treatment.csv



Update README with installation instructions

title: Differentially expressed genes across all cell types (Adu vs IgG)
text:
Code
library(qs2)
library(Seurat)
library(purrr)
library(DESeq2)
library(patchwork)
library(gridExtra)
library(pheatmap)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)

source("20260217-helper_functions.R")

# Load full object and microglia object (Kai's canonical annotation)
full    <- qs_read("seurat_objects/20260217_Part2_fullobj.qs2")
mg_kai  <- UpdateSeuratObject(qs_read("seurat_objects/20260225-mg_kai.qs2"))

# Discover all cell types
cell_types <- sort(unique(full$annotatedclusters))

title: Differentially expressed genes across all cell types (Adu vs IgG)
Differentially expressed genes across all cell types (Adu vs IgG)
Differentially expressed genes across all cell types (Adu vs IgG)
Astrocyte subpopulations
text:
Code
library(qs2)
library(Seurat)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)



astro <- qs2::qs_read("seurat_objects/20260305-astro.qs2")
Code
# Load full object 
full    <- qs_read("seurat_objects/20260217_Part2_fullobj.qs2")
astro0  <- subset(full, subset = annotatedclusters == "Astrocyte")
# Keep only the assay needed for fresh SCTransform
DefaultAssay(astro0) <- "Xenium"
astro0 <- DietSeurat(astro0, assays = "Xenium", dimreducs = NULL, graphs = NULL)
# Drop Xenium image/FOV segmentation data; otherwise merge triggers huge future globals
astro0@images <- list()

# SCTransform on all astrocytes together (no split/merge, no integration)
astro <- astro0
astro <- SCTransform(
  astro,
  assay = "Xenium",
  new.assay.name = "SCT",
  verbose = FALSE
)

title: Astrocyte subpopulations
text:
PCA and dimensionality selection


Code
DefaultAssay(astro) <- "SCT"
astro <- RunPCA(
  astro,
  assay   = "SCT",
  features = VariableFeatures(astro),
  verbose = FALSE)




Code
# Elbow plot to visualise variance explained per PC
ElbowPlot(astro, ndims = 50) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 30, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Elbow plot") +
  theme_minimal()











Code
# PCA biplots coloured by sample and treatment
p1 <- DimPlot(astro, reduction = "pca", group.by = "Slide") +
  labs(title = "PCA — Slide")
p2 <- DimPlot(astro, reduction = "pca", group.by = "Treatment.Group") +
  labs(title = "PCA — Treatment")
p1 + p2











Code
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(1234)

# Compute nearest neighbors using first 30 PCs
astro <- FindNeighbors(astro, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30, graph.name = "astro_snn")

# Perform clustering at multiple resolutions for later comparison
astro <- FindClusters(astro, graph.name = "astro_snn", resolution = 0.1)

# Compute UMAP for visualization
astro <- RunUMAP(astro, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

qs_save(astro, "seurat_objects/20260305-astro.qs2")


resolution = 0.1


Code
#|fig-width: 5
#|fig-height: 5
#| label: plot UMAPs 1
DimPlot(astro)











Code
#| label: plot UMAPs 2
DimPlot(astro, split.by = "Treatment.Group")

title: Astrocyte subpopulations
text:
Top marker genes per cluster
FindAllMArkers run on log-normalized residual expression values from SCTransform in the SCT assay (Values are continuous and typically centered around 0)


Code
Idents(astro) <- astro$seurat_clusters
DefaultAssay(astro) <- "SCT"
all_markers <- FindAllMarkers(astro, only.pos = TRUE, densify = TRUE)
# write_csv(all_markers, file = "seurat_objects/20260305-astrocyte_all_markers.csv")




Code
top <- read_csv("seurat_objects/20260305-astrocyte_all_markers.csv") |> 
  group_by(cluster) |>
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC),.by_group = TRUE) |> 
  top_n(5, avg_log2FC) |> 
  pull(gene)

############################# violin plots ###########
library(gtable)
library(grid)

genes_of_interest <- unique(top)
violin_data <- FetchData(astro, c(genes_of_interest, "seurat_clusters"), assay = "SCT")
violin_data_long <- violin_data |>
    pivot_longer(-seurat_clusters) |>
    mutate(seurat_clusters = fct_rev(seurat_clusters),
           name = factor(name, levels = genes_of_interest))

# Get the default ggplot discrete colors
cluster_levels <- levels(violin_data_long$seurat_clusters)
palette_colors <- scales::hue_pal()(length(cluster_levels))
names(palette_colors) <- cluster_levels

p <- ggplot(violin_data_long, aes(x = seurat_clusters, y = value, fill = seurat_clusters)) +
    geom_violin(aes(color = seurat_clusters),
                scale = "width", trim = TRUE, adjust = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = palette_colors) +
    facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Expression Levels") +
    theme(
        legend.position = "left",
        # Color each y-axis label to match its cluster
        axis.text.y = element_text(
            size = 12,
            color = rev(palette_colors[cluster_levels])
        ),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "italic")
    )

# Convert to grob and remove duplicate y-axes from all facets except the first
g <- ggplotGrob(p)
axis_grobs <- which(grepl("axis-l", g$layout$name))
invisible(
  map(axis_grobs, function(i) {
    if (g$layout[i, "name"] != "axis-l-1-1") {
      g$grobs[[i]] <<- nullGrob()
    }
  })
)

grid.newpage()
grid.draw(g)
